excerpt: The Imāms, the Purified Ones, have clarified the proper method of ghusl: one must first perform the preliminary ablution (wuḍūʾ), then cleanse away any visible impurity. Thereafter, water must be poured over the entire body such that every surface receives the stream—from the crown of the head to the soles of the feet.

The Commander of the Faithful declares that believers must never surrender themselves to sleep in a state of impurity or without performing ablution. Should water be unavailable, one performs the dry ablution (tayammum). This is because the soul of the faithful ascends toward the heavens during sleep, and the Almighty regards it with loving attention.
source_location_hint: Discourse on the soul and sleep

excerpt: The ghusl of Friday (ghusl al-Jumu'a), by virtue of its supreme rank among the twelve recommended baths, stands as their leader. It is the first of the sunna baths just as the ritual bathing of the deceased is the last among the obligatory baths. This position reflects a profound correspondence: the Friday ghusl serves as a bridge between obligatory and recommended purifications.
source_location_hint: On the ranks of ritual practices

excerpt: All these defects arise from weakness in faith (īmān), negligence in worship, indifference to knowledge, and corruption in love for the Imāms. Such a person has not inhabited the world of wisdom, has not adorned the assembly of virtues, nor has he established contemplation in his innermost sanctuary. The water of Friday's ghusl washes away all these outward and inward stains from body and soul alike.
source_location_hint: On the purification of the soul through ritual acts

excerpt: Nails should be trimmed at the time of the Friday ghusl. Both the visible nails of the hands and feet, as well as the hidden aspects of the self, require pruning. Nails are the outermost extremities of the body where impurity collects; they signify external attachments and beliefs that obscure the truth.
source_location_hint: On the etiquettes of Friday preparation

excerpt: When a knowledgeable preacher (dāʿī) imparts learning to a seeker (mustajīb), both must afterward wholly attribute this knowledge to the Source of Truth—the Imām al-Zamān—and distance themselves from any claim of personal authorship. The one who speaks with the permission of the Imām is like one who lawfully gathers sustenance; the one who speaks religious truths without his permission is like one committing forbidden intercourse.
source_location_hint: On the principles of esoteric exegesis (taʾwīl)

excerpt: When a believer is obliged to perform ghusl, his relationship with the Nāṭiq (the Speaker, i.e., the Messenger) is temporarily severed because the real bond has been broken by impurity. On account of this severance, the angels withdraw from him. Proper and timely ghusl restores the connection with the angel.
source_location_hint: On the relationship between ritual purity and angelic protection

excerpt: When a person forms the intention to perform ablution or ghusl and pronounces 'Bismillāh ar-Raḥmān ar-Raḥīm,' the water must be engaged at that very moment. The components governing inner purity become activated and perform their function of purifying the soul of the believer. These components are fashioned from the knowledge of the Imāms.
source_location_hint: On the metaphysical properties of water and intention

excerpt: We are bound by periods and cycles. In the past, we performed acts of goodness toward those who dwelt in unbelief—deeds now forgotten by us, yet preserved by the nature of all things. God brings forth to us their condensed essence in the form of water, and we perform ablution with it. God possesses the reckoning of every particle.
source_location_hint: On the cosmic reckoning and the nature of water

excerpt: The Islamic water is administered by Fāṭima, the Mistress; the water of faith is administered by the Imām al-Zamān and ʿAlī ibn Abī Ṭālib. Pure water contains components of the knowledge of the Imāms. In the world of life, there is the Mother of Waters (Umm al-Māʾ)—the Rising Water—which mingles with the waters of this earthly realm.
source_location_hint: On the cosmic waters and the Imāms

excerpt: The soul of the seeker, owing to the absence of any formed knowledge, is weak. Because of this weakness, his soul accepts any knowledge indiscriminately—whether from truth or falsehood. This moral ugliness of the seeker stems from ignorance of his eternal world. The insignificant and disordered thoughts of such a seeker resemble menstrual blood: they accumulate, yet because they are weak and cannot solidify, they shatter at the slightest objection.
source_location_hint: On the spiritual interpretation of menstruation

excerpt: When the seeker receives the knowledge of a true teacher and forms a concept of truth, that concept absorbs all his doubts and annihilates them. Just as the physical form of the child is perfected through menstrual blood, so the spiritual form of the seeker is perfected through the inner knowledge of the preacher, and this form, once established, is never lost.

Once he has taken the pledge (performed the menstrual ghusl) and has acquired correct knowledge of the ranks and limits of religion, it now becomes his duty to conceal these ranks from the undeserving—something he could not have done before. This is the spiritual meaning of fasting.
source_location_hint: On the concealment of religious knowledge

excerpt: The more a scholarly conception breaks through and dispels doubt, the greater the perfection and stability that emerges in the seeker's faith. One true conception overcomes countless doubts, and that precious embryonic knowledge, which possessed no status, emerges as a perfected human being.
source_location_hint: On the transmutation of doubt through knowledge

excerpt: The second emanation assumed an equality with conjecture—the opposite of menstruation—and sought to make itself equal to the First Intellect, which led to its fall into error. When the second being repented and acknowledged the greatness and superiority of the limits of the first, its error was removed, and it returned to the domain of the man.
source_location_hint: On the emanations and their spiritual ranks
